description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in wooden shingles, with a prominent stone chimney rising from one side. The exterior walls are a mix of exposed timber framing and plaster, with a stone foundation visible at the base. Several small, multi-pane windows are set into the upper and lower levels, some with decorative wooden trim. The front includes a covered porch supported by wooden posts, and the rear has an attached lean-to structure with a stone oven and barrel. The overall design is rustic, evoking a medieval or Tudor-style cottage, with weathered wood and stone textures throughout. The house appears compact but detailed, with architectural elements that suggest both functionality and historical charm.
description: A rustic, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, stone chimney, and a mix of wood, plaster, and stone textures. Features include a covered porch, small multi-pane windows, and a rear lean-to with a stone oven.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main gable, covered porch, central window, and timber-framed facade.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the symmetrical roof, two dormer windows, and the stone foundation.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back porch, balcony, and the large stone chimney.
description_view_3: Side view with attached lean-to, stone oven, barrel, and a small window under the sloped roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with small multi-pane windows and dormers
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with wooden posts, central door, and small steps leading up to the entry
